data.title: Consumer confidence unexpectedly strengthens in March
data.description:
U.S. Consumer Confidence Index: 91.8 in March vs. 88.5 consensus and 91.0 (revised from 91.2) prior, according to data released by The Conference Board on Tuesday.

Present Situation Index: 123.3, up by 4.6 points vs. February.

Expectations Index: 70.9, down by 1.7 points from the prior month.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Ton Strategy outlines focus on increasing TON held per share as it reports $356.8m in digital assets at year-end 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TON Strategy Company (TONX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Manuel Stotz (Executive Chairman) positioned the company as a public-market vehicle to “hold Toncoin to stake a substantial portion of the position and to increase TON held per share over time inside a public company structure,” arguing TON is “designed for real economic utility and activity inside the Telegram ecosystem, where more than 1 billion users already communicate, transact and engage with digital services.”
  • Stotz (Executive Chairman) emphasized why the structure matters for U.S. investors, saying the company is designed to provide “transparency, discipline and access to that exposure,” and added, “We believe our structure is particularly relevant now while direct access to TON remains more limited in U.S. markets.”
  • Stotz (Executive Chairman) described 2025 execution steps and the period of ramp, stating, “In August, we raised capital, established our initial position and began staking,” and added that Q4 was “the first full fiscal period with staking in place, which is giving us a better view of how the model operates with the operating infrastructure fully established.”
  • Stotz (Executive Chairman) reiterated the leadership transition status: “the company continues to conduct an active search for a permanent CEO as part of a planned leadership transition,” and noted, “Veronika continues to serve as CEO during this transition.”
  • Sarah Olsen (CFO & COO) framed 2025 as a blend of the new treasury strategy and legacy operations: “our 2025 results reflect both the implementation of our TON treasury strategy beginning in August as well as the contribution of the company's legacy operating businesses.”

Outlook

  • The call did not include explicit EPS or revenue guidance.
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) described an operating priority around treasury and spending discipline, saying, “Going forward, operationally, our emphasis will remain on disciplined treasury management,” including “preserving appropriate liquidity and financial flexibility,” and added, “we are applying that same discipline to our cost structure, including careful expense management and a continued focus on operating efficiently.”
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) signaled ongoing disclosures focused on treasury metrics: “We expect to continue updating most company reported treasury metrics through our regular quarterly and annual public filings, consistent with our long-term treasury approach.”

Financial Results

  • Olsen (CFO & COO) reported full-year 2025 total revenue of $12.8 million (vs. $0.9 million in 2024), stating it “included approximately $4 million from staking activities following the implementation of the TON treasury strategy.”
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) reported gross profit of $7.6 million (vs. $0.7 million in 2024) and total costs and expenses of $49.2 million (vs. $12.5 million in 2024), attributing the increase “primarily due to noncash stock-based compensation expense, treasury implementation costs and costs associated with the infrastructure to support custody, staking, reporting and compliance.”
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) reported loss from operations of $36.4 million (vs. $11.6 million in 2024) and net loss before income taxes of $148.6 million (vs. $10.5 million in 2024), adding that net loss “included a $114.2 million net loss on crypto assets, which reflects realized and unrealized fair value changes in Toncoin Holdings during the year.”
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) disclosed balance sheet levels at year-end: “At December 31, 2025, digital assets held a fair value of approximately $356.8 million and cash and restricted cash totaled approximately $39.7 million.”
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) provided staking activity metrics and positioned them as an operating proof point: “As of year-end, we had 219.7 million tons staked, and we earned 2.19 million tons since taking implementation.”

Q&A

  • No analyst Q&A session was included in this transcript.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment could not be assessed because no analyst Q&A was included in the transcript.
  • Management sentiment was positive and execution-focused in prepared remarks, with Stotz (Executive Chairman) stating, “we entered 2026, having moved through the initial launch phase,” and emphasizing, “our core focus remains on disciplined execution.”
  • A quarter-over-quarter sentiment comparison was not possible because the prior-quarter transcript was not provided beyond a “not available” placeholder.

Quarter-over-quarter Comparison

  • The prior-quarter transcript was not provided beyond “not available,” so guidance language, strategic emphasis, key metric changes, and tone shifts could not be compared.

Risks and Concerns

  • Olsen (CFO & COO) tied a major 2025 loss driver to market-driven Toncoin valuation changes, stating that results “included a $114.2 million net loss on crypto assets,” reflecting “realized and unrealized fair value changes in Toncoin Holdings.”
  • Stotz (Executive Chairman) reiterated an ongoing CEO transition, saying the company is conducting “an active search for a permanent CEO,” while “Veronika continues to serve as CEO during this transition.”
  • Olsen (CFO & COO) described liquidity and flexibility as explicit operating considerations alongside staking: “disciplined treasury management” includes “preserving appropriate liquidity and financial flexibility.”

Final Takeaway

Management described TONX as a public-company structure built to hold and stake Toncoin with the stated goal to “increase TON held per share over time,” highlighting that 2025 included the initial buildout (capital raise, initial position, and staking) and that Q4 was the first full period with staking in place. The company’s reported results reflected both staking contribution and significant volatility tied to Toncoin fair value changes, while management emphasized ongoing transparency through filings, a newly launched dashboard, and a continued focus on disciplined treasury management and cost control alongside the ongoing CEO search.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: WeRide, Uber launch robotaxi service in Dubai
data.description:
WeRide (WRD) launched fully driverless robotaxi service in Dubai through the Uber (UBER) app as part of the first batch of Level 4 robotaxis to enter commercial service in the city.

Service began today in Umm Suqeim and Jumeirah districts, two of Dubai’s most popular tourist destinations.

Riders can be matched with a WeRide (WRD) robotaxi through the ‘autonomous’ option on the Uber (UBER) app.

In February of this year, WeRide (WRD) and Uber (UBER) committed to deploying at least 1,200 robotaxis across the Middle East, spanning Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Riyadh with 200 currently in service.

The Dubai deployment comes on the heels of Uber’s (UBER) recent disclosure that it had acquired a 5.8% passive stake in WeRide (WRD). The investment is part of a strategic partnership between the two to expand WeRide (WRD) robotaxis to 15 cities over five years.

Building on gains tied to the Uber (UBER) investment, WeRide (WRD) shares are up another 13% Tuesday, bringing its two-day gain to 17%.

data.title: Nike options chain shows upside-heavy positioning into earnings
data.description:
Options traders are positioning for a significant move in Nike (NKE) shares following the company’s earnings report after Tuesday's market close, with the bulk of speculative interest skewed toward the upside.

The April 2 weekly options chain is pricing in roughly an 8% to 9% post-earnings swing, translating to about $4.30 in either direction from current levels. Based on near-the-money straddles in the 50 to 52 range, the market is effectively valuing NKE in the low $51s as of Monday's close, implying a potential earnings-driven range from around $47 on the downside to roughly $55.50 on the upside.

What stands out is the heavy call concentration above the current stock price. The largest call open interest sits at the 54 strike with 6,050 contracts, followed by the 55 strike at 5,186 contracts and the 60 strike at 3,988 contracts. Monday’s trading volume reinforced this pattern, with particularly active buying in the 54, 55, and 56 calls.

Overall, call volume and open interest ran well ahead of puts, suggesting traders are leaning more heavily into upside participation than pure downside protection.

On the put side, the primary hedge zone appears concentrated around the 49 to 50 strikes. The 50 put carries the largest put open interest at 4,487 contracts, while some traders maintain tail protection at the 45 strike with 2,626 contracts open.

The positioning suggests the options market sees the real battleground in the low-to-mid $50s rather than anticipating a deep selloff, though traders remain braced for a sizable move in either direction.

Nike (NKE) advanced 1.8% to $52.17 a share in Tuesday morning trading. The stock closed at $51.24 on Monday.

Key themes investors will be focused on include the update from Nike (NKE) on current demand trends, especially in China, and the outlook for margins amid new headwinds. In addition, management commentary on product innovation, inventory discipline, and collaborations will tip sentiment. The impact of the World Cup being hosted in the U.S. this summer could also be a catalyst.

data.title: U.S. equities rally as Trump signals potential wind-down of Iran war
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages pushed higher on Wednesday as an end to the Iran conflict could be near, with President Donald Trump prepared to wind down the military campaign against the country within weeks.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +1.3%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +1%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, ten of the 11 S&P segments were in the green, with consumer discretionary at the top. At the other end of the spectrum, utilities have suffered the most. 

Treasury yields edged lower despite a jump in March across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) dropped 5 basis points to 3.78%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) declined 2 basis points to 4.89%.

Trump also urged countries hit by Strait of Hormuz fuel disruptions to buy from the U.S. or “take it” themselves in remarks highlighting tensions with allies over the Iran war.

The Wall Street Journal reported that Trump told aides he is willing to end hostilities on a four-to-six-week timeline, prioritizing the degradation of Iran’s navy and missile stocks over the more complex mission of reopening the Strait of Hormuz. 

On the economic calendar, investors will soon see Chicago PMI and JOLTS data land. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Marvell Technology (MRVL) climbed +7.2%, while shares of Biogen (BIIB) dropped -4.3%.

data.title: Chicago PMI falls more than expected in March
data.description: The Chicago PMI fell to 52.8 in March from 57.7 in the prior month, against the 54.0 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Tuesday.
